stories.headline: Iran's IRGC declares Strait of Hormuz closed to all vessels after second night of US strikes; CENTCOM disputes it
stories.summary: Early on June 11, Iran's IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to all vessels including oil tankers, warning any ship attempting passage would be fired on, and said it had struck two ships. US CENTCOM disputed the closure, saying the strait remains open and commercial traffic continues. Brent crude pushed above $96 a barrel after a second night of US strikes on Iranian coastal sites.

perspectives.keyFraming: Iran's closure declaration collides with US insistence the waterway is open, leaving the actual status of a chokepoint for a fifth of global oil contested.
perspectives.summary: Al Jazeera and Seatrade Maritime reported the IRGC declared the strait closed and claimed it hit two ships, while US officials said the waterway remained open and vessels were still transiting. Roughly 20% of the world's seaborne oil normally moves through Hormuz, so even an unverified closure claim carried an immediate war premium into crude prices.

sources.title: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan and Hormuz ships after new US strikes
Iran claims it hit two ships, declares Strait of Hormuz closed

perspectives.keyFraming: Iranian and Russian state media present the closure as a sovereign security measure forced on Tehran by repeated US attacks.
perspectives.summary: TASS relayed Iran's top military command declaring the strait shut to all traffic, framing it as a legitimate defensive response to US strikes on Iranian territory rather than an offensive blockade. State coverage emphasized Iranian resolve and warned foreign vessels away.

sources.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz for all vessels — top brass

perspectives.keyFraming: Gulf-region coverage foregrounds the maritime and energy-trade fallout of a contested closure on the region's economies.
perspectives.summary: Gulf News reported Iran's declaration that the strait was closed to all ships and oil tankers, stressing the consequences for the roughly fifth of global oil and much of the world's LNG that transits the waterway. Regional outlets highlighted shipping disruption and insurance costs over great-power messaging.

sources.title: Iran declares Hormuz Strait 'closed' to all ships, oil tankers

stories.headline: Trump threatens to widen Iran campaign to power plants as US launches second day of strikes; Hegseth says 'negotiate with bombs'
stories.summary: President Trump said the US carried out a second day of strikes on Iran on June 10 and threatened to expand the campaign to power plants and bridges, telling Fox News 'I may keep going' as talks stalled. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the US would 'negotiate with bombs' if needed. CENTCOM said the strikes answered Iran's downing of a US Apache and Iranian fire on US-hosting bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan.

perspectives.keyFraming: The administration frames the renewed strikes as calibrated self-defense and as leverage to force Tehran into a deal.
perspectives.summary: CENTCOM said the strikes were a response to Iran's continued aggression, and Defense Secretary Hegseth told reporters that if the US needs to 'negotiate with bombs, we'll negotiate with bombs.' Officials cast the operation as proportional retaliation for the downed Apache and Iranian attacks on bases hosting US troops.

sources.title: Iran Updates: Hegseth says U.S. will "negotiate with bombs" if needed, as U.S. military announces more strikes
U.S. military says it's striking 'multiple targets' in Iran in 2nd day of renewed fire

perspectives.keyFraming: Renewed back-and-forth strikes are testing a fragile two-month ceasefire and risk widening into a regional war.
perspectives.summary: Al Jazeera reported it was the third time in a week that strikes tested the ceasefire, with the second day of US action following Iranian fire on Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan, all of which host US forces. The account noted Trump's warning that Tehran would 'pay the price' for stalled negotiations and Iran's vow to stand firm.

sources.title: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan and Hormuz ships after new US strikes

perspectives.keyFraming: Conservative coverage casts Trump as projecting strength against a defeated Iran that is stalling for time.
perspectives.summary: The Times of Israel and CNBC reported Trump saying Iran had 'taken too long' to agree to a deal and would now 'pay the price,' with the president declaring Iran's military 'a complete and total mess.' Coverage framed the second day of strikes as Trump putting Tehran on notice after what he called repeated stalling.

sources.title: Trump: US will be attacking Iran again today, 'they keep playing us for suckers'
Trump says Iran will 'pay the price' and claims they have 'taken too long' to agree to a deal

stories.headline: Oil extends gains as second day of US strikes on Iran and Hormuz closure claim tighten supply; Brent tops $96
stories.summary: Crude rose for a second straight session on June 11 as the US launched fresh strikes on Iran and Tehran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed, with Brent climbing above $96 a barrel and WTI toward $92. EIA data showed US crude inventories fell 7.2 million barrels last week, a seventh consecutive weekly draw. US Energy Secretary Chris Wright said some Gulf vessel traffic and exports were nonetheless rising.

perspectives.keyFraming: The move is supply-driven: a seventh straight US inventory draw plus the Hormuz closure claim kept a structural bid under crude even as some Gulf exports continued.
perspectives.summary: Reuters-sourced reporting via Investing.com framed the roughly 1-2% gain as supply-led, citing the second day of strikes and seven consecutive weekly US inventory declines tightening balances. OilPrice.com reported Brent above $96 and WTI near $92 as the war premium returned, with the Strait of Hormuz still effectively closed.

sources.title: Oil rises nearly 1% as US launches new strikes against Iran, supply tightens
Oil Prices Jump After U.S. Strikes Iranian Missile Sites

perspectives.keyFraming: US officials stress that exports and Gulf traffic are recovering, casting the strikes as self-defense rather than a deliberate energy shock.
perspectives.summary: OilPrice.com relayed US Energy Secretary Chris Wright's message that vessel traffic and Strait exports were rising despite the disruption, presenting the strikes as proportional responses to the downing of a US helicopter. The administration line sought to reassure markets that physical flows were not being fully severed even as Iran declared the strait closed.

sources.title: Strait of Hormuz Constraints Keep Oil Prices Elevated

stories.headline: Cairo Gaza talks stall over Hamas disarmament as factions weigh partial handover of weapons to a new body
stories.summary: Egyptian-mediated talks in Cairo with Egypt, Qatar and Turkey reached an impasse, with sources calling Hamas disarmament the 'only point of contention.' Palestinian factions reportedly agreed in principle to transfer part of their weapons to a yet-to-be-formed temporary body, but Hamas ties decommissioning to an Israeli withdrawal while Israel demands full disarmament first.

perspectives.keyFraming: Israeli-oriented coverage casts disarmament as the last obstacle, with Israel insisting on full Hamas disarmament before any further steps.
perspectives.summary: The Times of Israel reported the Cairo talks stalled with Hamas disarmament described as the only remaining point of contention. It said Israel demands full disarmament up front, while Hamas conditions any handover of weapons on an Israeli withdrawal, leaving mediators searching for a sequencing compromise.

sources.title: Gaza talks hosted by Egypt stall as Hamas disarmament 'remains only point of contention'

perspectives.keyFraming: Regional coverage highlights the factions' in-principle willingness to hand over part of their arsenal to a temporary national body as a step forward.
perspectives.summary: Pakistan Today reported that Palestinian factions discussed transferring part of their weapons stockpiles to a yet-to-be-formed interim body, framing the move as a concession that keeps the process alive despite the deadlock over full disarmament. The account stressed mediators' efforts to bridge the gap rather than the collapse of talks.

sources.title: Palestinian factions discuss partial Gaza disarmament in Cairo talks
